title: tectaria variolosa
intro: Tectaria variolosa, also known as the Variable Fern or St. Christopher's Fern, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant is used in traditional medicine, particularly in the Philippines and other parts of Asia, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Tectaria variolosa include treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as being used as a natural remedy for wounds and snake bites. In herbal preparations, the leaves of Tectaria variolosa can be made into tea, or the roots can be used to create a decoction for medicinal purposes.
